name: loudcheck
description: Loudness compliance verdict against formal published standards (EBU R128, ATSC A/85). Measures integrated loudness, loudness range, and true peak via ffmpeg, returns pass/fail with per-metric deltas and exact remediation — a compliance answer, not raw meter output.

exit_codes.0: pass — file complies with the standard
exit_codes.1: fail — non-compliant; remediation included in output
exit_codes.2: error — missing file, no audio stream, ffmpeg absent, unknown standard

output.json_flag: --json emits the full verdict object on stdout

shape.failures: plain-English causes, one per failed metric
shape.remediation: exact corrections, e.g. 'apply -2.3 LU gain ... loudnorm I=-23'

output.errors: Error:-prefixed text on stderr, never a traceback

tools: check_loudness(path, standard='EBU_R128') -> verdict dict (identical to CLI --json)
list_standards() -> catalog with targets, tolerances, citations

scope.in: formal, stable standards only (EBU R128, ATSC A/85; PRs adding formal standards welcome as pure data)
scope.out: per-platform delivery templates (Netflix/DPP/...), loudness correction, full-file QC, real-time monitoring
